sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: West Ham United have had mixed results in their last 5 matches, with a strong 3-0 win against Nottingham Forest but heavy losses like 1-5 to Chelsea. Tottenham Hotspur also show inconsistency, with a 3-0 win over Burnley but a recent 0-1 loss to Bournemouth. Both teams struggle defensively, conceding frequently in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, the results were split with a 1-1 draw and a 4-1 win for Tottenham. Both teams have scored in their recent encounters, suggesting a trend of open, high-scoring games between them.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham (17th) have scored 4 goals but conceded 8, showing defensive vulnerabilities. Tottenham (7th) also have a similar goal tally but a better defensive record with only 1 goal conceded in league play. Tottenham's superior league position and form suggest a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: West Ham's Jarrod Bowen is in form with 2 goals and 2 assists recently, posing a threat. Tottenham often score first in matches (5/7) and have a strong record of no losses in recent head-to-heads (3 games). Both teams' games often see high corners (over 10.5 in 5/6 head-to-heads) and low cards (under 4.5 in most matches).

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at the London Stadium between West Ham United and Tottenham Hotspur ended in a goalless draw. Neither side managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Tottenham controlled more of the ball and showed greater intent in the attacking third, while West Ham remained compact and looked to exploit counter-attacking opportunities. Both teams had limited clear-cut chances, with defenses largely holding firm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Tottenham Hotspur dominated possession and territorial advantage in the first half, frequently penetrating the final third and creating pressure through set pieces. Their approach play was more structured, often finding spaces in dangerous areas near West Ham's penalty box. West Ham, on the other hand, were more reserved but showed resilience in defensive duels, particularly in the air, where they outperformed their opponents. The Hammers relied on quick transitions but struggled to turn these into meaningful goal-scoring opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The attacking momentum shifted several times throughout the first half. Tottenham started strongly with sustained pressure in the opening minutes, maintaining a consistent threat in the early stages. West Ham responded with brief spells of attacking intent around the middle of the half, pushing forward with purpose and challenging Tottenham's defense. However, as the half progressed, Tottenham regained control with prolonged periods of dominance, especially in the closing minutes, piling on pressure and looking the more likely to score before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Lucas Paquetá stood out as a potential game-changer with his ability to create from deeper positions, showing glimpses of quality in tight spaces. Jarrod Bowen also remains a threat on the flank with his direct running. On the Tottenham side, Pedro Porro has been influential with his overlapping runs and delivery from wide areas. Pape Matar Sarr and Cristian Romero also posed problems with their involvement in attacking plays, particularly from set pieces.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights for the second half
halftimeSections.text: West Ham may need to take more risks in the second half by pushing higher up the pitch to disrupt Tottenham's rhythm and create chances of their own. Their defensive solidity has been commendable, but they lack the cutting edge in the final third. Tottenham, meanwhile, should continue to exploit their numerical advantage in the attacking zones and focus on converting their territorial dominance into goals. Their ability to win corners and create pressure could be decisive if they maintain this intensity.
